site_tagline: Instrument any terminal program — observe, control, and render it.
site_description: tappty hosts a program on a pseudo-terminal, fans its output to any number of observers — a window, a socket, an automated driver — and routes input back, so a human and a bot can watch and drive the very same session.

docs.title: Command guide
docs.summary: The tapterm command in depth: the CUI / GUI / web / headless modes, the terminal model, recordings, snapshots, and recipes.

docs.title: Programming reference
docs.summary: The library API: Session, the five Sources, both Terminal backends, the bus, the renderers and compositor, with the shared contracts.

docs.title: Architecture & design
docs.summary: How it works inside and why: the Source → Terminal → Session → renderer/bus pipeline, concurrency, the trust model, and the known limits.

docs.title: Gallery
docs.summary: See it in action — screenshots of color, the digital-rain animation, and the compositor, each with runnable source.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.summary: Dated history, newest first: when tappty started, when it first worked, and what has changed since.
